step_description: Tests whether strategy and growth planning has a documented end-to-end design covering planning inputs, decision points, ownership, cadence, and approval rules, with clear ownership, decision rights, and handoff rules.

Tests whether strategy and growth planning operates consistently in practice and whether planning cycles happen on time, use the right inputs, and translate into committed actions without avoidable delay, rework, or dependency on individual heroics.

Tests whether strategy and growth planning is controlled through clear metrics such as on-time planning cycle completion, initiative milestone attainment, and variance to plan, with timely exception visibility, review cadence, and corrective action ownership.

Tests whether customer and segment definition has a documented end-to-end design covering segment criteria, customer definitions, qualification logic, and ownership of updates, with clear ownership, decision rights, and handoff rules.

Tests whether customer and segment definition operates consistently in practice and whether teams consistently use the same customer and segment definitions in targeting, qualification, and reporting without avoidable delay, rework, or dependency on individual heroics.

Tests whether customer and segment definition is controlled through clear metrics such as qualified lead rate by segment, win rate by segment, and revenue mix by segment, with timely exception visibility, review cadence, and corrective action ownership.

Tests whether offer and service design is controlled through clear metrics such as gross margin by offer, delivery variance, and exception rate to standard, with timely exception visibility, review cadence, and corrective action ownership.
